title: Pentair (PNR) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Pentair (PNR) compiled by Gemini.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 6% growth rate reflects steady, normalized growth. Following the volatility of the pandemic boom and subsequent destocking bust, Pentair returns to its historical trajectory driven by consistent aftermarket replacement demand, modest price increases, and mid-single-digit volume growth in water solutions.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9% discount rate is appropriate for a high-quality, predictable industrial company with a strong balance sheet and defensive end-markets, reflecting a lower-than-average risk profile.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal growth rate assumes Pentair will grow consistently in line with global GDP, bolstered by the permanent necessity of clean water and ongoing infrastructure needs.

thesisTitle: A High-Quality Compounder Trading at Fair Value
thesisParagraphs: Pentair represents a quintessential high-quality industrial business. Its focus on water treatment and pool equipment provides a highly predictable revenue stream, heavily skewed toward aftermarket replacements rather than cyclical new construction. The company's massive installed base acts as a powerful economic moat, generating robust and consistent free cash flow across various macroeconomic environments. While the stock suffered during the post-pandemic inventory correction, operations have normalized, and the long-term secular tailwinds of water scarcity and energy efficiency remain intact.

Our DCF analysis indicates that Pentair is currently trading near its intrinsic value, offering a modest 5.5% margin of safety. While it may not present a deep-value opportunity, the company is fairly priced for the predictable, low-risk compounding it provides. For long-term investors, Pentair serves as a reliable, defensive anchor holding that consistently returns capital through a growing dividend, avoiding the boom-and-bust cycles typical of broader industrial manufacturing.

faq.question: Is the residential pool market recovery fully priced in?
faq.answer: Largely, yes. The market has recognized the end of the destocking cycle, which is why the stock trades near our calculated fair value rather than at a steep discount.

faq.question: Why is Pentair considered a defensive stock?
faq.answer: Water treatment and pool maintenance are largely non-discretionary. If a pool pump breaks or a commercial water filter fails, it must be replaced immediately, regardless of the broader economic climate.

faq.question: Does Pentair's dividend impact the valuation?
faq.answer: The dividend is a product of the strong free cash flow we model in the DCF. The company's long history of increasing dividends highlights management's discipline and the underlying stability of the cash generation.
